Do not merge the two lines — enslaved ancestry narrative belongs on François's branch per current evidence, not on the GRP-0011 chain, unless a later marriage between branches is proofed.

interpretation.african_ancestry_notes: Direct enslaved-line documentation (screenshots) extends before Rosette/Jeanne naming alone: Francois (St. Denis) + Fanny (GRP-0331/0332) → Marie Jeanne (GRP-0330), on the Francois/Pelagie branch (GRP-0040/0042). That narrative does not automatically ride the father-to-father GRP-0011→GRP-0021 line unless a later union is proofed. Parish extracts and 1779 contract images remain the legal standard — this file tracks research, not final civil status.

truth_pass.jeanne_marie_anne_merge: Strong working hypothesis: Marie Anne Therese Francois de la Grande Terre and Jeanne Guyon dit Despres et d'Herbanne (GRP-0101) are one woman under multiple colonial labels (Jeanne, Marie Anne Therese, de la Grande Terre, Guyon/Despres/d'Herbanne). Not treated as two women in this file until registers disprove merge.
truth_pass.louise_prior_marriage_besson: Locked as strong working lineage fact: Louise Marguerite Guedon married Jean Baptiste II Besson (GRP-0110), was widowed, then married Alexis Grappe dit Touline (GRP-0001) in 1746 — explains Besson-ordered children before Grappe children. Besson offspring not individually keyed here.
truth_pass.place_corridor: Ignore noisy geocoder labels (e.g. Minnesota, Garza TX, Fort St. Louis/Texarkana mismatches). Default normalization: Natchitoches Post / Natchitoches Parish / Campti / Cane River / Isle Brevelle corridor unless a primary record fixes a different locality.
truth_pass.ignore_without_posterity: Treat online-tree 'not married / without posterity' as filler, not proof, unless sourced.
truth_pass.francois_sr_vs_jr: Keep Francois Grappe Sr. (GRP-0040, 1747, d. 1825/1829) distinct from Francois Grappe Jr. (GRP-0051, c.1788/1789); death-year variants on Sr. are compilation noise, not a second man.
truth_pass.jacques_grappe_francois_rosatte_child: Jacques Grappe 1763–1848 (GRP-0055) is modeled as a child of Francois Grappe + Marie Louise Rosette per Rosette-facing records — not as a half-sibling cluster from corrupted GEDCOM.
truth_pass.hinge_manuel_vs_marcelite: Path A: Manuel Perot (GRP-0214) toward dit Perot / Dorthlon if naming stays Perot-heavy. Path B: Marcelite (GRP-0213) toward Julia Pailette Dortolon / Dortolon if the line runs that way. Both remain open until parish proof.

truth_pass.alexis_marriage_alliance_note: Treat marriage to Louise as strategic, not merely domestic: trade access, military alignment, territorial influence, kinship alliance between French authority and entrenched local (Guedon) networks — fits Natchitoches / upper Red River frontier politics (working narrative).
truth_pass.backbone_doc_upper_river_power_household: Build one tight document before full Rosette expansion: Upper River Power Household — Guedon → Besson (widowhood) → Grappe (alliance); Louise as regional node; Francois upper-river childhood (use Sibley deposition: until age 16–17 at ancient Caddo village); Rosette entry and oral-history manumission lore. Feeds lineage truth model and novel household politics.
truth_pass.francois_1805_sibley_deposition: Primary anchor: Francis Grappe of Campti, age 57, sworn 22 Sep 1805 before John Sibley JP — born near ancient Caddo village on Red River (~500 river miles above Natchitoches); resided there until age 16–17; father commandant, fort built by governor's order; trader witness to upper Red settlements. Cole-Jett 2024 transcribes. Supersedes informal 'age 13' oral.
truth_pass.rosette_enslaved_maternal_anchor: Repeated Ancestry/screenshot structure: Marie Louise Rosette dit Souris Jeanne / Jeanneton Mezieres — labels consistent with enslaved/free Creole documentation (e.g. Dupre). Not a 'hidden' branch: she is the documented maternal pivot for Rosette-generation children; parish extracts still required for every legal claim.
truth_pass.emmanuel_lucas_chronology_note: Emmanuel Lucas b.1801 is modeled as child of Marie Pelagie Grappe b.1766 (GRP-0042) + Jean Chrysostome Perot (GRP-0203). UI timelines that highlight 'Pelagie (1790)' are a different sibling (GRP-0052) — do not use her as Emmanuel's mother.
truth_pass.st_denis_jeanne_fanny_line: Screenshots name Marie Jeanne's parents as Francois (St. Denis slave) and Fanny — multi-generation enslaved line before Rosette-era labels. GRP-0330 (Marie Jeanne) + GRP-0331/0332 hold this subgraph; GRP-0042 still uses Model A parents in JSON until parish merge proves Rosette = Marie Jeanne or successive filiation.
truth_pass.marriage_contract_1779_note: User cites a 1779 contract as status/property evidence (enslaved listed as assets, Trichel/Fontenot network). Add exact archive citation, parish, and transcription when available.
truth_pass.alexis_jean_b_vs_francois_fork: Working structure: both GRP-0040 (François Baptiste Touline Grappe) and GRP-0011 (Jean B Grappe) are sons of Alexis (GRP-0001) + Louise (GRP-0002). Marie Jeanne / Rosette / St Denis screenshot chain is tied to François's household and Pelagie (GRP-0042), not to the straight paternal ids GRP-0011→GRP-0021. Extended-family kinship and later Trichel/Perot overlaps are real; genetic contribution from François's branch into Jean B's descendants requires a documented union, not assumption.

people.notes: First marriage to Louise Marguerite Guedon (GRP-0002), 1741; she was widowed before marriage to Alexis. Besson-line children not individually keyed in this file.

Born 1717 Besancon, Doubs, France (profile). Married 9 Apr 1746 Natchitoches; died 10 Jan 1776 Natchitoches. Preserve dit Touline. Union with widowed Louise framed as frontier alliance (see interpretation.truth_pass.alexis_marriage_alliance_note). Nine named slots GRP-0003–0010 + Jean B (0011); Francois/Rosette line tracked as GRP-0040.

Prior marriage to Besson and widowhood before Alexis are treated as strong working facts (see power_household). Profile lists nine children (slots GRP-0003–0010 + GRP-0011); Francois (GRP-0040) is the Rosette household line.

power_household.marriage_to_alexis_function: Frontier alliance and power consolidation: trade access, military alignment, territorial influence, kinship tie between French colonial authority and entrenched local (Guedon) networks — not merely a household union.
power_household.oral_history_rosette_freedom: Strong oral-history hypothesis (not documentary lock): Louise-associated household strategy may have shaped Marie Rosette's path into the family, including lore of manumission mediated through that household — explains Rosette's centrality despite enslavement-tinged origins in the record trail.
power_household.matriarch_role: Widow, alliance-maker, household strategist, mother of a frontier-formed son (Francois); regional power node — not only 'wife of Alexis'.

people.source_basis: Household list + 1805 sworn deposition (Sibley) via Cole-Jett digest
people.notes: Also cited as Francois Baptiste Touline Grappe. Death year also given as 1829 in some trees; spouse Marie Louise Rosette. Wired to seed-person-francois. Keep distinct from son Francois Jr. (GRP-0051). Working sibling: Jean B Grappe GRP-0011 (user paternal line) — same parents GRP-0001+0002. Sept 1805: 'Francis Grappe, of Campte,' aged 57 → birth ~1748 (aligns with 1747 here). Identity with the deponent is a strong working match — confirm with christening record if found. Testimony describes father as post commandant and French fort context; does not name mother (Rosette not in this document).

formative_context.childhood_region: Born near the ancient Caddo village on the Red River, 'upwards of five hundred miles above Natchitoches' by the course of the river (1805 deposition); Nassonite / upper Caddo area per Robin Cole-Jett digest — not Campti birthplace.
formative_context.raised_until_age_note: Sworn 1805: parents lived there until he was sixteen or seventeen years of age (supersedes earlier oral approximate '13').
formative_context.identity_profile: Long-time trader; lived at Campti by 1805; childhood in French military-civilian post at ancient Caddo village — father's commandant role, small fort with two cannon, French flag, agriculture like lower Louisiana, mill stones moved 1778. Frontier-formed witness to upper Red River French settlements.
formative_context.primary_source_citation: John Sibley, justice of the peace, Natchitoches, 22 September 1805. Sworn deposition of Francis Grappe of Campte; reported e.g. National Intelligencer (Washington DC).
formative_context.secondary_article_citation: Robin Cole-Jett, 'Testimony by Francois Grappe about the French along the Red River in New Orleans Territory,' 24 Sep 2024 (transcription and interpretation).
formative_context.primary_text_anchor: Personally appeared before me, John Sibley … Francis Grappe, of Campte … aged fifty-seven … he was born near the ancient Caddo village on the Red river, which by the course of the river he believes to be upwards of five hundred miles above Natchitoches, where his parents then lived … until he was sixteen or seventeen years of age. … his father was commandant of the place for many years … his father, by order of the governor of Louisiana, built a small fort there, in which two small pieces of cannon …
